# SolarSubsidies.com — Vendor Partnership Agreement

**Version 1.0 · Effective Date: [DATE]**

---

This Vendor Partnership Agreement ("Agreement") is entered into between:

**SolarSubsidies.com** (operated by [LEGAL ENTITY NAME], a [proprietorship/private limited] registered at [ADDRESS], India), hereinafter referred to as "Platform"

AND

**[VENDOR LEGAL NAME]** (GSTIN: [GSTIN], PAN: [PAN], registered at [VENDOR ADDRESS]), hereinafter referred to as "Vendor"

The Platform and the Vendor are individually referred to as "Party" and collectively as "Parties."

---

## 1. PURPOSE OF THIS AGREEMENT

The Platform operates a website at solarsubsidies.com that provides solar subsidy information to residential, commercial, and agricultural customers in Uttar Pradesh, India. The Platform refers qualified, pre-screened customer leads to selected installer vendors in exchange for a commission on closed deals.

This Agreement defines the terms under which the Vendor will receive customer leads, the obligations of both Parties, and the commission structure.

---

## 2. VENDOR ELIGIBILITY (REPRESENTATIONS)

The Vendor represents and warrants the following are true as of the date of this Agreement and will remain true during its term:

a) Vendor holds active MNRE empanellment (Number: [MNRE_NUMBER]) and the empanellment is in good standing.

b) Vendor holds active UPNEDA approved-vendor status (Number: [UPNEDA_NUMBER]) and the approval is in good standing.

c) Vendor has a valid GSTIN ([GSTIN]) and PAN ([PAN]).

d) Vendor has completed a minimum of 25 residential rooftop solar installations in the last 24 months, verifiable through UPNEDA service records.

e) Vendor has an in-house installation team (not solely sub-contracted) capable of executing the volume of leads they accept.

f) Vendor uses only BIS-certified solar panels and CEA-approved inverters in all installations.

g) Vendor has no active material consumer complaints on NCDRC or UPNEDA helpdesk.

The Vendor authorizes the Platform to verify these representations through official MNRE, UPNEDA, GST, and consumer-complaint databases at any time during the term of this Agreement.

---

## 3. LEAD ASSIGNMENT AND ROUTING

### 3.1 Lead Definition

A "Lead" is a record in the Platform's system containing at minimum: customer name, phone number, district, monthly electricity bill, system size preference, property type, intent, and timeline. Each Lead is assigned a unique Lead Match ID for tracking.

### 3.2 Routing Method

The Platform will route Leads to Vendors based on:
- Match between customer district and Vendor's declared service districts
- Match between system size and Vendor's minimum size threshold
- Match between property type and Vendor's declared property types
- Vendor's current capacity utilization vs. declared weekly capacity
- Vendor's tier (premium tier receives priority routing for HOT leads)

### 3.3 Lead Exclusivity

Each Lead is assigned exclusively to one Vendor at a time. No Lead will be simultaneously routed to multiple Vendors. If a Vendor declines or fails to respond within 24 hours, the Platform may reassign the Lead to another Vendor.

### 3.4 Vendor Response SLA

The Vendor agrees to:
- Acknowledge each assigned Lead within **4 business hours** of assignment (WhatsApp, call, or email)
- Make initial contact with the customer within 24 hours
- Schedule a site visit within 7 days if the customer is interested

Repeated failure to meet the 4-hour acknowledgment SLA for HOT-tier leads is grounds for tier demotion or removal from the network.

---

## 4. COMMISSION STRUCTURE

### 4.1 Commission Rate

The Vendor agrees to pay the Platform a commission of:
- **7% (seven percent)** of the gross system value during the probationary period (first 6 months from agreement signing)
- **7% or 8%** thereafter, based on Vendor's performance tier (see Section 5)

The commission is calculated on the **gross system value** (the customer-facing system price before any government subsidies are applied), not on net cost.

### 4.2 Commission Trigger

Commission becomes payable upon **net meter activation** by the relevant DISCOM, confirming that the installation is complete and operational. Commission is **not** payable on:
- Contract signing alone
- System installation that has not yet been net-meter-activated
- Customer cancellations before installation completion
- Leads where the Vendor was not the assigned party

### 4.3 Invoice and Payment Terms

The Platform will issue an invoice to the Vendor within 7 (seven) days of net meter activation. Payment terms are **net-30 (thirty days)** from invoice date. Payment may be made via NEFT, UPI, RTGS, or cheque.

Late payments accrue interest at 1.5% per month on the outstanding balance after 30 days from due date.

### 4.4 Commission Disputes

In case of dispute regarding whether a Lead was successfully closed by the Vendor, the following constitutes binding evidence:
- Customer confirmation via WhatsApp or SMS of installation completion with Vendor name and date
- UPNEDA service record showing installation linked to the customer's address
- Vendor's invoice issued to the customer

Disputes are resolved through good-faith discussion within 14 days. Unresolved disputes are subject to arbitration in Lucknow, UP, under the Arbitration and Conciliation Act, 1996.

---

## 5. PERFORMANCE TIERS

### 5.1 Probationary Tier (Months 1-6)

All new Vendors begin in the Probationary tier with a 7% commission rate. The Platform tracks:
- Average response time to assigned Leads
- Close rate (leads closed / leads assigned)
- Customer satisfaction (post-installation survey)
- Quote-to-installation conversion time

### 5.2 Standard Tier (Months 6+)

Vendors meeting baseline performance metrics (close rate ≥15%, average response time <8 hours, customer satisfaction ≥4.0/5) move to Standard tier with 7% commission and continued lead access.

### 5.3 Premium Tier (Top 30%)

Vendors in the top 30% by close rate and customer satisfaction may be promoted to Premium tier with:
- 8% commission rate (vs 7% Standard)
- Priority routing of HOT-tier leads
- Featured listing on district pages

### 5.4 Suspension and Removal

The Platform may suspend or remove Vendors for:
- Repeated failure to meet response SLA (>3 incidents in 30 days)
- Customer complaints exceeding 10% of completed installations
- Loss of MNRE or UPNEDA approval
- Material breach of Section 6 (Vendor Obligations)
- Attempts to circumvent commission (Section 7)

---

## 6. VENDOR OBLIGATIONS

The Vendor agrees to:

a) **Quality:** Use only BIS-certified panels and CEA-approved inverters. Provide standard manufacturer warranties (typically 25 years for panels, 5-10 years for inverters) and honor them.

b) **Pricing transparency:** Provide written quotes specifying the gross system price, expected PM Surya Ghar subsidy, expected UPNEDA subsidy, and net cost. The Vendor sets its own prices but must disclose them clearly.

c) **Subsidy paperwork:** Complete PM Surya Ghar portal application on behalf of the customer (with their authorization) and coordinate with the DISCOM for net metering.

d) **Customer service:** Respond to customer queries during the installation cycle in a timely manner.

e) **Reporting:** Update lead outcomes in the Platform's vendor portal (when available) or via WhatsApp confirmation. Notify the Platform of net meter activation within 7 days of occurrence.

f) **Data protection:** Use customer data received from the Platform solely for the purpose of completing the installation. Do not sell, lease, or transfer customer data to third parties. Comply with the Digital Personal Data Protection Act, 2023.

g) **No cross-referrals:** Do not direct Platform-referred customers to non-Platform vendor partnerships or attempt to convince customers to "bypass" the Platform.

---

## 7. PROHIBITION ON COMMISSION CIRCUMVENTION

### 7.1 Prohibited Acts

The Vendor agrees NOT to:

a) Refuse a Platform-assigned Lead and then re-engage the same customer through another channel within 90 days to avoid commission.

b) Claim that a Platform-assigned Lead originated from a different source (e.g., "Justdial" or "walk-in") to avoid commission.

c) Direct Platform customers to relatives, subsidiaries, or partner vendors not in the Platform network.

d) Offer Platform customers a discount conditional on bypassing the Platform.

### 7.2 Consequences of Circumvention

Documented commission circumvention results in:
- Immediate suspension from the Platform
- Charge of 2× the standard commission on the bypassed installation as liquidated damages
- Reporting to MNRE and UPNEDA (the Platform reserves the right to inform these bodies of vendor misconduct that affects scheme integrity)

---

## 8. TERM AND TERMINATION

### 8.1 Term

This Agreement is effective from [DATE] and continues until terminated by either Party.

### 8.2 Termination by Vendor

The Vendor may terminate this Agreement with 30 days' written notice (email to vendors@solarsubsidies.com is sufficient). Outstanding commissions on closed deals remain payable.

### 8.3 Termination by Platform

The Platform may terminate this Agreement immediately for material breach (Section 5.4) or with 30 days' notice without cause.

### 8.4 Effect of Termination

Upon termination:
- No new Leads will be routed to the Vendor
- Existing assigned Leads in progress remain with the Vendor, with commission obligations intact
- Customer data shared with the Vendor must be retained or destroyed per the customer's instructions

---

## 9. CONFIDENTIALITY

Both Parties agree to maintain confidentiality of:
- Customer personal data
- Platform's lead scoring algorithm and routing logic
- Vendor's pricing, sales volume, and operational data shared during the relationship

This obligation survives termination of the Agreement.

---

## 10. NON-EXCLUSIVITY

This Agreement is non-exclusive. The Vendor is free to:
- Engage with other lead-generation platforms (Justdial, Sulekha, etc.)
- Run its own marketing channels (Google Ads, Facebook, direct sales)
- Service customers acquired through any other channel

The Platform makes no commitment regarding minimum lead volume.

---

## 11. INDEMNIFICATION

The Vendor agrees to indemnify and hold harmless the Platform from any claims arising from:
- Defective installations performed by the Vendor
- Misrepresentation of product warranties
- Customer complaints related to Vendor's workmanship or service
- Violations of consumer protection or DPDP Act laws by the Vendor

The Platform indemnifies the Vendor from claims arising from inaccurate or misleading information published on solarsubsidies.com that the Vendor relied upon in good faith.

---

## 12. LIMITATION OF LIABILITY

Neither Party shall be liable for indirect, incidental, or consequential damages. The total cumulative liability of either Party under this Agreement is limited to the total commissions paid or owed during the preceding 12 months.

---

## 13. DISPUTE RESOLUTION

a) **Negotiation:** Parties shall first attempt to resolve any dispute through good-faith negotiation for 14 days.

b) **Arbitration:** Unresolved disputes are submitted to arbitration in Lucknow, Uttar Pradesh, under the Arbitration and Conciliation Act, 1996. The arbitration shall be conducted in English. The arbitration panel shall consist of one mutually agreed arbitrator.

c) **Governing law:** This Agreement is governed by the laws of India. Courts in Lucknow have exclusive jurisdiction.

---

## 14. AMENDMENTS

The Platform may update commission rates, tier criteria, and operational policies with 30 days' written notice. Material changes to commission structure require Vendor's written agreement before applying.

---

## 15. ENTIRE AGREEMENT

This Agreement constitutes the entire understanding between the Parties and supersedes any prior agreements or representations.

---
